islamicCalendar.title: Islamic Calendar
islamicCalendar.descIslamicNewYear: The first day of Muharram marks the beginning of the Islamic New Year. A time for reflection and gratitude.
islamicCalendar.descDayOfAshura: The 10th of Muharram. A day of fasting and remembrance, commemorating the salvation of Prophet Musa (AS).
islamicCalendar.descMawlidAlNabi: Celebration of the birth of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH). A day to remember his life and teachings.
islamicCalendar.descIsraAndMiraj: The Night Journey and Ascension of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Makkah to Jerusalem and to the heavens.
islamicCalendar.descFirstDayOfRamadan: The beginning of the blessed month of fasting, prayer, and spiritual reflection.
islamicCalendar.descLaylatAlQadr: The Night of Decree, better than a thousand months. Seek it in the last ten nights of Ramadan.
islamicCalendar.descEidAlFitr: The Festival of Breaking the Fast. A joyous celebration marking the end of Ramadan.
islamicCalendar.descDayOfArafah: The 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah. Standing at Arafah is the essence of Hajj. Fasting on this day expiates sins.
islamicCalendar.descEidAlAdha: The Festival of Sacrifice. Commemorating Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) willingness to sacrifice his son.

banned.contactSupport: আপনি যদি মনে করেন এটি ভুল, তাহলে সিদ্ধান্তের বিরুদ্ধে আপিল করতে পারেন বা সাপোর্টে যোগাযোগ করতে পারেন।
